model.label: MiMo-V2-Pro
model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Arizona holds the superior overall profile as the #1 seed with elite metrics on both ends of the floor (KenPom/Torvik rank #3, offense rank 7-10, defense rank 3), while Purdue's nation-leading offense (rank 1-2) is offset by a weaker defense (rank 36). The Wildcats' balanced attack and defensive prowess should contain Purdue's offensive firepower, and their 31-2 record demonstrates consistency against top competition. While Purdue's offensive efficiency presents a significant challenge, Arizona's overall talent depth and defensive capability give them the edge in this Elite 8 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: West Regional Final: Arizona vs Purdue Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Arizona's superior overall balance and defensive strength give them an edge over Purdue's elite but one-dimensional offense.
evidence: Arizona ranks #3 in both KenPom and Torvik with a 31-2 record, featuring top-10 offense (rank 7-10) and top-3 defense (rank 3).
Purdue ranks #8 in both systems with a 26-8 record, featuring the nation's #1-2 offense but only #36 defense.
Arizona's defensive efficiency (adjde 91.1) is significantly better than Purdue's (adjde 100.2-100.5), creating a key matchup advantage.
Both teams have tournament experience, but Arizona's overall balance and defensive prowess provide a more reliable path to victory in a high-stakes Elite 8 game.

result.snippet: The No. 3-ranked Purdue basketball team got 75 combined points from Fletcher Loyer, Braden Smith and Zach Edey to win just its third game all-time against a No. 1-ranked team, with a 92 - 84 win over top-ranked Arizona .

result.snippet: No. 3 Purdue 92 , No. 1 Arizona 84 (Postgame Notes) • The No. 3-ranked Purdue men's basketball team improved to 10-1 overall with a 92 - 84 win over No. 1-ranked Arizona at the Indy Classic in Indianapolis. The win was Purdue's third straight victory.
